_notes: AUTHORED short (not carved from LF). CORRECTED FEC figures (2026-05-30). Angle: 'The money that LOST.' A friendly-named pro-Cornyn PAC spent $39.3M to STOP Ken Paxton; Paxton WON the May 26 primary anyway (~64%). Punchline lands on the backfire. NO VO — text cards + music + faceless atmosphere b-roll. All money/names via Remotion (navy CR brand) — never an AI face of a real person.
_no_vo_note: 2026-05-24 founder lock: shorts use text + music + b-roll only. No VO. See CR-PRODUCTION-PIPELINE-v4.md Doctrine line 4.

broll.prompt: Cozy living-room interior at dusk, an old glowing television set on a wooden stand, soft warm lamplight, empty armchair and couch in foreground, faceless wide shot, no people visible, no on-screen text, cinematic shallow depth of field, slow gentle push-in. Atmosphere only.

props.text: An ad rolls across your TV in Texas: “Texans for a Conservative Majority.”
Sounds like a crowd of your neighbors. It was one man.

props.quote: $39.3M in ads — not to lift their guy up, but to STOP one other Republican: Ken Paxton.

props.label: PAXTON WON ANYWAY — MAY 26 PRIMARY
props.caption: 64 out of every 100 votes, against $39M of attack ads

props.text: $39 million tried to stop one man. He won anyway. The biggest check in the race lost.
